summary:
Raising the temperature of the water during mixture preparation decreases the setting time of irreversible hydrocolloid impression materials such as alginate due to accelerated chemical reactions driven primarily through increased molecular motion and reactivity at higher temperatures.
The optimal range is typically between approximately 60-70°C but should not exceed boiling point or damage other components within dental procedures. To ensure accurate results while minimizing patient discomfort from prolonged handling times, practitioners are advised to use appropriately heated solutions when working with these types of materials under various conditions encountered clinically. Temperature adjustments offer a simple yet effective method to manage workflow efficiency without compromising material quality once set properly according to manufacturer guidelines after achieving The optimal range is typically between approximately 60-70°C but should not exceed boiling point or damage other components within dental procedures.
